failedAssertions.reason: The successful user.switchTeam mutation updated the shell to the secondary workspace before the dashboard body/privateData caught up, so the active workspace did not change immediately across the rendered UI.
Right after switching teams, the shell and protected dashboard content disagreed on the active workspace until a later re-render corrected the mismatch.

Invite recovery reached /dashboard with privateData for the invited workspace, but the shell lost the current-workspace indicator and the transition emitted Authentication required server-render errors.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented the low-memory tenancy round concurrency cap (1 browser validator) and the tenancy-specific fixture workflow, including which seeded users belong to browser versus API invite validation.
Added a reusable fixture seeder that provisions disposable tenancy-ux users, workspaces, memberships, and invite states for real API/browser validation against the dev database.
